title: pisophaca diehlii
intro: Pisophaca diehlii, also known as the Lupine, is a flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) that grows in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Pisophaca diehlii include its high concentration of alkaloids, such as sparteine, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Pisophaca diehlii can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as respiratory issues and skin problems.
